name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: Have a great Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
---
Peter,

As summer comes to an unofficial close, I wanted to take a moment to say thank you for your ongoing support and wish you a Happy Labor Day!

And as we celebrate Labor Day, let us remember the importance of American workers. Our nation's great economic strength, technological, and engineering achievements are owed in large part to the workers of yesterday and today.

However you choose to celebrate today, please enjoy a safe and happy Labor Day!

Thank you, _-Abigail_

name: The West Virginia Democratic Party
subject: Happy Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by the WV State Democratic Executive Committee (www
body:
[https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP02/1/67825/images/Red%2C%20White%20and%20Blue%20Photo%20Popular%20Quotes%20Fourth%20of%20July%20Instagram%20Post-2.png]

Fellow Democrat,

Today, we are celebrating Labor Day with our families and friends while paying tribute to the brave workers who came before us.

We honor those who gave their blood, sweat, and tears to secure the 8-hour workday, 40-hour workweek, paid leave and vacations, workplace safety protections, equal opportunity in the workplace, healthcare benefits, pensions, and so much more.

West Virginia workers know that organized labor is responsible for securing the protections and benefits we have today, and we stand united to protect them.

The Republican Leadership in West Virginia has led the charge against our unions and our workers, drawing back our workplace safety protections, slashing wages and health care, and denying workers and retirees of their hard earned benefits and pensions.

The self-serving GOP in West Virginia only cares about lining their own pockets and the pockets of their wealthy corporate buddies, and West Virginia workers foot the bill.

We at the West Virginia Democratic Party promise to continue to stand with hardworking families.

With 2020 just around the corner, we remain committed to voting out those who continue to attack our workers.

West Virginia workers have never turned their backs on each other, and we will never turn our backs on them.

Happy Labor Day!

-Team WVDP

name: Mary Mancini
subject: Labor Day Newsletter: Campaign training opportunities, Delegate selection updates and more!
body:
Dear Peter,

It's Labor Day 2019. And since there are no off years for Democrats, it's a good weekend to get active, celebrate the contributions of the labor movement, and remember that we deserve and must seek out leaders who will have our back.

We deserve and must seek leaders who will side with working families instead of relying on race-based strategies of division to keep us fighting amongst ourselves.

We deserve and must seek leaders who will embrace our Tennessee values that we look out for each other - whether we're white, black or brown, tenth generation or newcomer. Our leaders must have our back.

We deserve and must seek leaders who understand we are all Tennesseans who want the same things - a fair day’s pay for a fair day’s work, access to affordable health care, an education that fits our vision for a better future, an affordable place to live, and a secure and dignified retirement.

**And today we must remind each other of what the labor movement has meant to the United States and when workers have a seat at the table, they have the power to make the economy work for more Tennesseans.**

Sincerely,

\--
Mary Mancini
Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party

* * *
**THE INTERSECTION OF UNION VALUES AND HEALTH CARE**

Last week, the Tennesse Democratic Party concluded our Stand For Rural Healthcare Tour, which visited communities throughout the state most impacted by rural hospital closures and lack of access to health insurance due to the Republican refusal to expand Medicaid. Today, as we celebrate the Labor movement's history and contributions to American prosperity, we want to share with you a testomony from Polk County, which lost Copper Basin Medical Center in 2017.

Cindy Moss, one of the first babies born at Copper Basin Medical Center, is the daughter of a union miner who helped pay for the hospital to be built.

_"The men who worked the mines were proud to belong to the union. In 1956, one of the things they decided to do through their union was pool their money together to build a modern, community hospital to serve the common good. Those men had seen their co-workers killed and maimed in the mines. They saw all those little gravestones of all the children who died and the women who died in childbirth. These were Americans who volunteered to contribute to the common cause and to their community. That's where this hospital came from."_

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/9579382/94859829/-620374283?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzEzNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NjUzZThjOC04MGNkLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QjrUgQriHY2G2GMcva2agmzAAH_2kmL_X1Ubwl07cJY=&__xts__\[0\]=68.ARDHCX3OeT_4-oxqHabKKHnSLmNJhPl1VZcKyi_6VvDOIBe8svnKNsDvlHw4FI6NQTYxIl7ULrf9EZcPSa-MANBbLd1rjp6ylIDIHI45YsMnx3bCLmihglxg81tLtXLpgEzQ9IbG707xj24X5ZoqBVdxUpoK_qyYdmyNqrqai2VGHwD0cGuFugYYt2cJWwQvzjiaD8siPlWLnkXIiigC6roPPQtjZFTCOLf5uR6GuI_cQu4Tm589SdNyAl4GLPISyugr1w-DIb7vfbfYKMWt_pc0uDHQrePw1z5I6mdD6wkeXESCAO2gOVb9nZepQ98d8uOQERaoZx1PBxXBcv0E4ycFUcwockIQ&__tn__=-R)

Her remarks are a sobering reminder of the shared values that unites Democrats and the labor movement, as well as the reality that when labor unions lose power, the quality of life for Tennesseans diminishes.

Our message during the tour echoed the belief of the copper miners that built Copper Basin Medical Center hospital in 1956: Healthcare and hospitals are an essential pillar for any healthy community and exist to serve the public good, not as means for hospital monolopies to extract money out of our communities and cut off services the minute they can no longer do so.

* * *
**FALL 2019 GRASSROOTS TRAINING OPPORTUNITY**
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/9579383/94859830/-1350364669?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzEzNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NjUzZThjOC04MGNkLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QjrUgQriHY2G2GMcva2agmzAAH_2kmL_X1Ubwl07cJY=)
The Tennessee Democratic Democratic Party, together with the DNC and Association of State Democratic Committees Best Practices Institute, are excited to announce the launch of our 2019 Train the Trainer Volume 2 (T3 v.2) Webinar Training Program!

T3 v.2 is a free three-week, six-part webinar course, covering several important aspects of grassroots campaigning from fundraising to volunteer recruitment, digital mobilization and grassroots organizing. This program seeks to expand the skills of progressive activists and volunteers, by ensuring that comprehensive training is free and accessible for Democrats all over the country.

**[Click here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/9579384/94859831/1749446341?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzEzNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NjUzZThjOC04MGNkLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QjrUgQriHY2G2GMcva2agmzAAH_2kmL_X1Ubwl07cJY=)** to learn more about the T3 v.2 Training program, and to complete your registration before the September 22 deadline. T3 v.2 trainings are held every Tuesday and Thursday at 7:00 p.m. ET, from September 24 through October 10. **[Registration](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/9579385/94859832/1749446341?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzEzNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NjUzZThjOC04MGNkLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QjrUgQriHY2G2GMcva2agmzAAH_2kmL_X1Ubwl07cJY=)** will close at 11:59 p.m. ET, on Sunday, September 22.

* * *
**2020 DNC DELEGATE SELECTION**
The very nature of the Democratic Party is that everyday folks from every walk of life and every corner of our Nation can come together to represent their communities. In this spirit, the Democratic voters of each state send a delegation to the DNC Convention to select the next President of the United States of America. This is the essence and purpose of our national convention.
Any dedicated Tennessee Democrat who wants to serve your community, county, district, and state can become a delegate to the 2020 convention, which will be held in Milwaukee the week of July 13, 2020, by running for election from your Congressional District or by running for an At-Large position. Your participation in the political system is the key to our democracy.
Democrats wishing to become delegates to the Democratic National Convention must file a **Declaration of Candidacy and Presidential Pledge of Support** form with the Tennessee Democratic Party office. Forms will be available online at [tndp.org](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/9579386/94859833/961702967?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzEzNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NjUzZThjOC04MGNkLWU5MTEtYmNkMC0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QjrUgQriHY2G2GMcva2agmzAAH_2kmL_X1Ubwl07cJY=) or from the TNDP office beginning January 6, 2020. Then, after the Tennessee Presidential Primary on March 3, 2020, all delegate candidates must attend their County Convention on March 7, 2020 to become a county selector, and then must attend their Congressional District Convention on March 21, 2020, which is when the Democratic selectors elected at each County Convention will meet to elect the 42 district-level delegates. On April 18, 2020 the TNDP State Executive Committee will elect the remaining members of Tennessee’s delegation: 8 Party Leader and Elected Official (PLEO) delegates, 14 At-Large Delegates, and 5 At-Large Alternates. Yeah, it's a process.
[Email Maria Brewer](mailto:maria@tndp.org?subject=Question%20About%20Delegate%20Selection), TNDP Director of Party Affairs, with any questions.

* * *
**TNDP LEAD 2020 PODCAST WITH TN AFL-CIO SECRETARY-TREASURER A.J. STARLING**

This month, we were proud to welcome Tennessee AFL-CIO Secretary-Treasurer AJ Starling onto the LEAD 2020 Podcast to discuss the rich history and partnership between organized labor and the Tennessee Democratic Party.
The LEAD 2020 Podcast is available on Spotify, Apple Podcasts and at tndp.org/podcast!
Tennessee Democratic Party
319 Plus Park Blvd
Nashville, TN 37217
United States

name: Team Beshear
subject: Labor Day note
body:
Labor Day isn't just a great excuse to fire up the barbecue and spend time with our friends and family -- it also gives us a chance to come together and celebrate the working men and women of Kentucky
|

| | Peter--

name: Michael (personal)
subject: words to live by
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for America
body:
| | | ["Labor Day symbolizes our determination to achieve an economic freedom for the average man which will give his political freedom reality." -Franklin D. Roosevelt](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbac27/134673f2/bc342a5/69962999/2368920433/VEsH/?g=GUSlGlH8x8Vcy9-bgCeB4mA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/octoberdebate?refcode=EM_FR_B1_090219_AC_ND&recurring=auto&amount=1&amounts=10,35,50,100,250,500,1000")

Team,

I've always been inspired by President Franklin Roosevelt. During the greatest crisis faced by our nation since the Civil War, FDR summoned the best of government to restore a country crippled by depression and the devastating hardship of millions of working families.

One of Roosevelt's greatest achievements was his consistent support for workers' rights and the role of unions in building a strong workforce that, in turn, created economic freedom for millions of Americans.

_" It is one of the characteristics of a free and democratic nation that it have free and independent labor unions."_

Today, in these challenging times, Roosevelt's words have never been more apt.

As the Trump administration does all it can to suppress workers' rights while providing millionaires and billionaires with massive tax cuts, we need to invest in our workforce and strengthen unions fighting to keep our nation free and democratic.

We also can't forget it was FDR who recognized the importance and need for equality of women in the workplace by appointing the workers-rights advocate Frances Perkins as the first woman Secretary of Labor - and the first woman ever in a presidential cabinet.

[Team, that's the courage and commitment to hard-working Americans in every part of our country we need today, 86 years after FDR and Frances Perkins.](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbac27/134673f2/bc342a5/69962999/2368920433/VEsE/?g=GUSlGlH8x8Vcy9-bgCeB4mA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/octoberdebate?refcode=EM_FR_B1_090219_AC_ND&recurring=auto&amount=1&amounts=10,35,50,100,250,500,1000")

On this Labor Day 2019, let us renew our commitment to restoring a government that works for all people while leveling the economic playing field for America's working men and women.

[Team, I'm going to keep fighting for hard-working Americans in every part of our country. I hope you will join me.](https://secure.michaelbennet.com/page/m/35cbac27/134673f2/bc342a5/69962999/2368920433/VEsF/?g=GUSlGlH8x8Vcy9-bgCeB4mA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/octoberdebate?refcode=EM_FR_B1_090219_AC_ND&recurring=auto&amount=1&amounts=10,35,50,100,250,500,1000")

Michael

name: Cory Booker
subject: Our country is stronger when our workers can easily organize and join unions
body:
| | |
---
| Friend, I wouldn't be where I am today without the right to organize. My grandfather was a UAW worker and a secretary in the union. His ability to organize helped my family break out of poverty and get into the middle class. My grandmother is from a family of mineworkers in Iowa. And if it wasn't for those folks fighting for better wages, I wouldn't be where I am now. **I deeply believe that our country is stronger when our workers can easily organize and join unions.** In fact, our campaign workers in the first four states have unionized, which strengthens our campaign, our employees, and the benefits we offer -- and I couldn't be prouder of them. But right now in our country, unions are under attack from President Trump, Republicans in Congress, a conservative Supreme Court, and large corporations. Corporate power and wealth are growing while workers' wages are essentially stagnant. And we've seen so many Republican-led states undermine the ability of people to organize their workplaces. **The right to unionize is sacred in our country, and right now it 's under assault.** When I'm president, I'll make sure that workers are empowered again. Quality jobs are about so much more than a paycheck -- they're about dignity and economic justice. I promise that, under my leadership, the attacks on organized labor will stop and union power will expand. Happy Labor Day,
Cory P.S.**[If you're interested, check out my speech at the AFSCME Public Service Forum on the necessity of unions in our country, and pass it on.](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0b41/5fca0d19/72eb6675/2919b3da/322350268/VEsH/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://twitter.com/CoryBooker/status/1168544598618230792")**

name: Tim
subject: In solidarity
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for America
body:
|
---
On this day we celebrate the contributions, perseverance, and hard work of workers across our country.

America is exceptional because of the contributions of workers throughout our history. And our nation stays exceptional because of their achievements today. We owe a debt of gratitude to past workers who fought for safety and dignity in the workplace.

From folks who assemble cars and appliances, to people who stock and bag groceries, and workers who build and maintain our roads and infrastructure, today we celebrate the Americans who know the value and difficulty of a hard day's work.

The United States is more prosperous now than ever, but working families are getting a raw deal. **If you work full time in our country, you should be able to afford a roof over your head and food for your family.** This isn't a radical idea** _\-- it 's the American Dream._**

As globalization and automation are on the rise, we need to commit ourselves to protecting and restoring the dignity of work -- to make sure that our sisters and brothers in labor don't get left behind.

So today, let's be sure to show gratitude to our hardworking brothers and sisters and commit ourselves to ensure they get their fair share of the fruits of their labors.

Thank you.

In solidarity,
Tim

|
---

name: Team Beto
subject: Beto’s NEW plan (Labor Day)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
This Labor Day, will you sign on to Beto’s plan to say you believe it's time for a new contract between workers, employers, and the government?
#
Too many people are working two or three jobs. Real wages have barely grown over the last forty years and almost a quarter of workers don’t earn enough to keep a family out of poverty. Beto is fighting for a **21st Century Labor Contract** , so that all workers:
* Will be able to join a union if that’s what they want;
* Will get paid a living wage regardless of industry;
* Will need just one job to take care of their family and have time for a fulfilling life outside of work without sacrificing economic security;
* Will be hired and paid based on what they do — not who they are;
* Will have access to the training they need in a changing labor market; and
* Will have access to a fair labor market.

[This Labor Day, will you sign on to Beto’s plan to say you believe it's time for a new contract between workers, employers, and the government?](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/beto-email-20190902/?source=ema20190902-52754&t=1&akid=52754%2E444211%2Ei_Vyvq)
**[Add your name](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/beto-email-20190902/?source=ema20190902-52754&t=2&akid=52754%2E444211%2Ei_Vyvq)**
The American economy is the largest in the world, but its opportunity remains out of reach for far too many. That’s what this 21st Century Labor Contract is all about. Please sign your name today if you’re with us.
\- Team Beto

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Celebrating Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
Labor groups are still fighting for an America that works for everyone, not just the wealthy and well-connected. They were on the front lines then, and they are on the front lines now | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/28529308/45c30650/139751167/6686f300/2700413774/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0=/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com?source=em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
---
Today's a federal holiday because on the eve of the 20th century, workers from across the country fought side by side and made their government recognize the importance of labor. Machinists, carpenters, and people who worked in mills, mines, factories, and more joined together to fight for better wages, better conditions, and a day to honor the power of working people.

The fight for labor continues today. Out on the trail, I've spoken with union members who are teachers, nurses, postal workers, home health aides, janitors, truck drivers, and more.

Labor groups are still fighting for an America that works for everyone, not just the wealthy and well-connected. They were on the front lines then, and they are on the front lines now.

This is a crucial moment in the fight for workers in our country. Right now, the rich and powerful are doing everything they can to break the backs of unions.

But unions aren't going away -- not today, not tomorrow, not ever. **Unions built America's middle class, and unions will rebuild America's middle class**. And I've got plans to rewrite the rules of our economy to give workers more power.

Together, let's make it easier to unionize. Let's give those unions more power, get the Department of Labor and National Labor Relations Board to actually look out for labor, pass a nationwide ban on so-called "right to work" laws, and more.

This is a crucial moment in the fight for workers in this country. It's a time for us to show whose side we're on. I'm proud to say that my campaign is unionized. All Democrats need to stand up and say, without hedging, that we support unions and we support legislation that will protect workers.

Throughout history, workers have gotten together and gotten organized. Kids went from working in factories to going to school. Americans went from working twelve hours a day, six days a week, to the 40-hour week we know now.

**We must remember that when working people fight, working people win. And today, fighting shoulder to shoulder for big, structural change is how we're going to create an economy that works for everyone.**

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth
